EMBASSY OF EAST MOUNTAIN

Overview

Address
101 EAST MOUNTAIN DRIVE, WILKES-BARRE, PA 18702
Phone
5708255892
Certified beds
120
Avg daily residents
101 (84% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1985-10-18
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.61 3.69 3.78 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.63 0.65 0.66 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.88 0.82 0.92 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.11 2.22 2.21 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.50 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.34
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.40
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.50
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
37.1%
annual
RN turnover
21.4%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2026-01-09 13 11 7 143 0 143
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2025-03-07 22 13 12 136 1 136
Total weighted health score
141.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (41)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
39 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. B 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. D 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2026-01-09 2026-02-21
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. E 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0680 Ensure the activities program is directed by a qualified professional. E 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. E 2026-01-09 2026-02-20
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-08-21 2025-09-18
0569 Notify each resident of certain balances and convey resident funds upon discharge, eviction, or death. D 2025-05-29 2025-06-05
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0790 Provide routine and 24-hour emergency dental care for each resident. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-11
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-04-12 2024-05-21
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-01-09 2024-02-20
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-01-09 2024-02-20
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-01-09 2024-02-20
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2023-09-27 2023-10-30
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2023-05-17 2023-07-06

Deficiencies 0812 — description Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. 0812.description

Deficiencies 0836 — description Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. 0836.description
